title: CodeBuddy Security Config
description: Controls what the CodeBuddy extension is allowed to do in your workspace — file access, terminal commands, network requests, and sensitive path blocking.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor autocompletion.

allowedPaths.description: Directories the extension is allowed to access. Each entry specifies a path and whether read-write is permitted.

path.description: Absolute or ~-relative path to an allowed directory.

allowReadWrite.description: true = read-write access, false = read-only. Defaults to false.

description.description: Human-readable note explaining why this path is allowed.

commandDenyPatterns.description: Regex patterns for terminal commands that should be blocked. Merged with built-in defaults.

items.description: A regex pattern (e.g. "rm\\s+-rf\\s+/") that matches commands to deny.

networkAllowPatterns.description: URL patterns the extension is permitted to fetch. If present, only matching URLs are allowed (allowlist mode).

items.description: A regex pattern matching allowed URLs.

networkDenyPatterns.description: URL patterns the extension must never fetch. Evaluated before allow patterns (denylist takes priority).

items.description: A regex pattern matching blocked URLs.

blockedPathPatterns.description: Glob or regex patterns for sensitive file/directory paths that should never be read or listed.

items.description: A pattern matching paths to block (e.g. ".env", "secrets/").
